note_data.title: Programming
keyPoints: Binary system is used to process data in logic gates/transistors, store data in registers, and process data on the computer
Denary uses base 10 system with digits 0 to 9, Binary uses base 2 system with 0s and 1s, Hexadecimal uses base 16 system with digits 0 to 9 and alphabets A to F
Hexadecimal is used in colour codes, error codes and IP & MAC addresses because it is easily understandable, easier to debug, and takes less space on the screen
Each shift left is equivalent to multiplying the binary number by 2^n (where n is the number of places shifted)
Each shift right is equivalent to dividing the binary number by 2^n (where n is the number of places shifted)
Two's complement format is used to represent negative binary numbers where the leftmost bit is changed to a negative value and determines the sign of the number
Overflow error occurs when the value is greater than the maximum value of the register
A character set is a list of all characters and symbols represented by a computer where each character and symbol has a unique value
A sound wave is sampled for sound to be converted to binary using an ADC by determining the amplitude of the sound after set intervals
The accuracy of a sound file increases with greater sample rates and sampling resolutions, but so does the file size

definitions.definition: A list of all characters and symbols represented by a computer
The result of carrying out a calculation that produces a value too large for the computer's allocated word size
Used by determining the amplitude of the sound after set intervals. This gives an approximate representation of the sound wave
The number of samples taken per second
The number of bits required per sample (i.e: bit depth)

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Provide actual code statements using correct syntax and variable names
Give detailed steps of what happens in the algorithm or code

modelAnswer.question: Write an algorithm to input 10 student marks from Karachi Grammar School, calculate the total and find the average mark.
modelAnswer.answer:
Total ← 0
FOR Count ← 1 TO 10
    INPUT Mark
    Total ← Total + Mark
NEXT Count
Average ← Total / 10
OUTPUT Average
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Use FOR loop from 1 to 10 with correct syntax
Mark 3: Input mark and add to running total inside loop
Mark 4: Calculate average by dividing total by 10

commonMistakes: If the leftmost 1 bits are lost while shifting to the left, an error will occur because the limit of the maximum number of left shifts possible will have been exceeded
If the rightmost 1 bits are lost while shifting to the right, an error will occur because the limit of the maximum number of right shifts possible will have been exceeded
The extra carry in the answer indicates that overflow error has occurred when the answer is greater than 255 in an 8-bit register
examinerTips: For binary to hexadecimal conversion, break into sets of 4 binary digits
When converting negative denary to binary in two's complement format, first write the positive value in binary, then invert all values, then add 1 to the inverted binary value
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Flowchart symbols: oval (start/end), rectangle (process), diamond (decision), parallelogram (input/output), with arrows showing program flow direction
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q1 (6 marks) — they asked students to explain the benefits of using procedures and parameters in programming, expecting modular programming concepts and code reusability
This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P2 Q4 (4 marks) — students had to match programming concepts like procedures, parameters, local variables and functions to their correct descriptions
This topic was examined in M/J 2024 P2 Q2 (5 marks) — candidates needed to describe input validation methods including range checks, type checks and loop structures with programming statements
This topic featured in O/N 2023 P2 Q3 (5 marks) — students wrote algorithms in pseudocode showing input validation, data processing and output formatting for a sponsorship program

This topic was tested in M/J 2022 P2 Q5 (6 marks) — they asked for three features of high-level languages that improve code readability, expecting meaningful identifiers, comments and modular structure

workedExample.question: Explain the benefits of using procedures and parameters in programming. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Define what a procedure is - a collection of programming statements grouped under a single identifier
Step 2: Explain modular programming benefit - allows code to be broken into smaller, manageable sections
Step 3: Describe reusability - procedures can be called multiple times without rewriting code
Step 4: Explain parameters - allow data to be passed into procedures making them flexible
Step 5: Mention maintenance benefit - easier to debug and modify individual procedures
Step 6: Note team development - different programmers can work on different procedures
workedExample.answer: Procedures enable programmers to write a collection of programming statements under a single identifier, allowing for modular programming where code is broken into manageable sections. They promote code reusability as the same procedure can be called multiple times without rewriting code. Parameters allow data to be passed into procedures, making them flexible and adaptable to different inputs. Procedures make programs easier to maintain and debug as individual sections can be modified independently. They also facilitate team development as different programmers can work on separate procedures simultaneously.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark each for: definition of procedure, modular programming benefit, code reusability, parameter functionality, maintenance advantage, team development benefit

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates failed to distinguish between procedures and functions' — always mention that functions return a value while procedures may not when comparing programming concepts

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often provided vague answers about code organization' — always give specific examples like meaningful variable names, detailed comments, and proper indentation when discussing program readability

Examiner Report 2024: 'Candidates frequently omitted validation loops in their algorithms' — always include WHILE or REPEAT loops for input validation, not just single IF statements for error checking
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw a flowchart showing input validation with decision diamonds for range checks (e.g., 1-14 for day number), type checks (integer/string validation), error message outputs, and loop structures that return to input when validation fails. The flowchart should show the complete validation process from initial input through successful validation to program continuation, including specific programming constructs like IF-THEN-ELSE statements and WHILE/REPEAT loops.
note_data.quickRecap: Binary system uses base 2 for computer processing while hexadecimal uses base 16 for more compact representation. Two's complement format represents negative numbers with the leftmost bit as negative. Overflow errors occur when calculations exceed the register's maximum value, and logical shifts multiply or divide by powers of 2.
